name: zscaler-client-connector
label: Zscaler Client Connector
description: Zscaler Client Connector™ is a lightweight agent for user endpoints, enabling hybrid work through secure, fast, reliable access to any app over any network.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.description: The service-based URL of the Zscaler Client Connector to which you connect and perform the automated operations.
fields.name: server_url
fields.tooltip: The service-based URL of the Zscaler Client Connector to which you connect and perform the automated operations.

fields.title: Client ID
fields.description: Unique ID of the Zscaler Client Connector that is used to create an authentication token required to access the API.
fields.name: client_id
fields.tooltip: Unique ID of the Zscaler Client Connector that is used to create an authentication token required to access the API.

fields.title: Client Secret
fields.description: Unique Client Secret of the Zscaler Client Connector that is used to create an authentication token required to access the API.
fields.name: client_secret
fields.tooltip: Unique Client Secret of the Zscaler Client Connector that is used to create an authentication token required to access the API.

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is set to True.
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is set to True.

operations.title: Get Enrolled Device List
operations.description: Retrieve a list of all enrolled devices of your organization and their basic details from Zscaler Client Connector based on input parameters that you have specified.

parameters.title: Username
parameters.description: Filter devices by enrolled user name in the Zscaler Client Connector portal to identify and manage devices associated with a specific user.
parameters.name: username
parameters.tooltip: Filter devices by enrolled user name in the Zscaler Client Connector portal to identify and manage devices associated with a specific user.

parameters.title: OS Type
parameters.description: Filter by device operating system type in the Zscaler Client Connector.
parameters.name: osType
parameters.tooltip: Filter by device operating system type in the Zscaler Client Connector.

parameters.title: Page Number
parameters.description: Specify the page number from which to retrieve results when pagination is applied. This parameter is used to fetch a specific subset of results based on the page number, helping to break large datasets into manageable chunks.
parameters.name: page
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page number from which to retrieve results when pagination is applied. This parameter is used to fetch a specific subset of results based on the page number, helping to break large datasets into manageable chunks.

parameters.title: Page Size
parameters.description: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return, per page, in the response. If not provided, the default page size is 50. The max page size is 5000.
parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.tooltip: Specify the maximum number of results this operation should return, per page, in the response. If not provided, the default page size is 50. The max page size is 5000.

operations.title: Get Enrolled Device Details
operations.description: Retrieve a device details of enrolled devices of your organization from Zscaler Client Connector based on input parameters that you have specified.

parameters.title: Unique Device Identifier (UDID)
parameters.description: Filter by unique device identifier in the Zscaler Client Connector.
parameters.name: udid
parameters.tooltip: Filter by unique device identifier in the Zscaler Client Connector.

parameters.title: Username
parameters.description: Filter devices by enrolled user name in the Zscaler Client Connector portal to identify and manage devices associated with a specific user.
parameters.name: username
parameters.tooltip: Filter devices by enrolled user name in the Zscaler Client Connector portal to identify and manage devices associated with a specific user.

operations.title: Get Device OTP
operations.description: Retrieve the one-time password (OTP) for a specific device. These single-use passwords are unique and tied to the device Unique Device Identifier (UDID). It is made of 10 random alphanumeric characters and can only be used once.

parameters.title: Unique Device Identifier (UDID)
parameters.description: Filter by unique device identifier in the Zscaler Client Connector.
parameters.name: udid
parameters.tooltip: Filter by unique device identifier in the Zscaler Client Connector.

operations.title: Get Device App Profile Password
operations.description: Retrieve the app profile password for a specific device. You can determine the assigned global passwords by using the username and os type of a device.

parameters.title: Username
parameters.description: Filter devices by enrolled user name in the Zscaler Client Connector portal to identify and manage devices associated with a specific user.
parameters.name: username
parameters.tooltip: Filter devices by enrolled user name in the Zscaler Client Connector portal to identify and manage devices associated with a specific user.

parameters.title: OS Type
parameters.description: Filter by device operating system type in the Zscaler Client Connector.
parameters.name: osType
parameters.tooltip: Filter by device operating system type in the Zscaler Client Connector.

operations.title: Download Device Details
operations.description: Downloads or exports device information as a CSV file from Zscaler Client Connector based on input parameters that you have specified.

parameters.title: OS Types
parameters.description: Filter the list of devices by operating systems. The default value is null, which represents ALL OS types. The following values represent different OS types: iOS, Android, Windows, macOS and Linux.
parameters.name: osTypes
parameters.tooltip: Filter the list of devices by operating systems. The default value is null, which represents ALL OS types. The following values represent different OS types: iOS, Android, Windows, macOS and Linux.

parameters.title: Registration Types
parameters.description: Filter the list of devices by registration types or states. The following values represent different device states: All States Except Removed, Registered, Removal Pending, Unregistered, Removed, and Quarantined.
parameters.name: registrationTypes
parameters.tooltip: Filter the list of devices by registration types or states. The following values represent different device states: All States Except Removed, Registered, Removal Pending, Unregistered, Removed, and Quarantined.

operations.title: Download Service Status of Devices
operations.description: Downloads service status of all devices as a CSV file from Zscaler Client Connector based on input parameters that you have specified.

parameters.title: OS Types
parameters.description: Filter the list by operating systems. The default value is null, which represents ALL OS types. The following values represent different OS types: iOS, Android, Windows, macOS and Linux.
parameters.name: osTypes
parameters.tooltip: Filter the list by operating systems. The default value is null, which represents ALL OS types. The following values represent different OS types: iOS, Android, Windows, macOS and Linux.

parameters.title: Registration Types
parameters.description: Filter the list by registration types or states. The following values represent different device states: All States Except Removed, Registered, Removal Pending, Unregistered, Removed, and Quarantined.
parameters.name: registrationTypes
parameters.tooltip: Filter the list by registration types or states. The following values represent different device states: All States Except Removed, Registered, Removal Pending, Unregistered, Removed, and Quarantined.

operations.title: Execute an API Request
operations.description: Sends an API request to an API endpoint based on specified HTTP method, endpoint, and other input parameters that you have specified, enabling flexible API interactions tailored to user needs.

parameters.name: method
parameters.title: HTTP Method
parameters.description:
Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options:  

DELETE 

GET 

PATCH 

POST 

PUT

parameters.title: Endpoint
parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.tooltip: Specify the target API URL path for example: /zcc/papi/public/v1/getDevices
parameters.description: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/images/pic.jpg, the endpoint would be /images/pic.jpg.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: query_params
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.

parameters.name: payload
parameters.title: Request Payload
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
